strategyPrinciples: Prioritize legal moves that improve immediate phase totals.
Do not auto-end turn solely because score is behind.
Preserve deterministic fallback behavior whenever model output is invalid.
rulesSummary: The goal is to have scored the most TRYs at the end of the match.
A match is 3 minutes and comprises of rounds.
Rounds comprises of several phases.
A single phase is 10 seconds.
Each player gets 1 phase before they begin contesting.
The player with the highest phase total wins the contest.
To increase your phase total, play cards.
You have 8 momentum per phase.
Cards cost momentum to play.
When a phase completes, player cards in play lose stamina.
Winning a phase pushes the ball position +1 in your favor.
A round is won when either player gets the ball position to +3 in their favor.
Winning a round scores you a TRY, giving you +5 points.
Whoever scored more TRYs (has the most points) wins the match.
There are 2 kinds of cards:
PLAYER -> Cost is in the top-right, which is also the card's POWER.
Usually has a special effect, except for PROP.
When on the field, has a stamina bar at the top of the card.
When the stamina bar turns red, they are on their last phase and will be
removed from play the following turn.
TACTIC/PLAY -> Cost is in the middle-left. Requires you have a PLAYER card
on the field. Performs a special effect when played.
May be limited to 1 per phase, such as DRIVE.
Winning a phase pushes the ball position +1.

heuristics: Play when it improves immediate projected phase total.

source_lines: Player —Anchor —2 —Lose 1 less STA when you lose a phase.

Best when trailing on score/phase pressure.
source_lines: Play —Counter Ruck —2 —If you lost last phase, gain +3 PWR this phase.

heuristics: Boosts temporary skill and phase total immediately.
Strong when board already has at least one player.
Use once per turn to shift ball position toward attack.
Avoid wasting when already at best ball position.
source_lines: May be limited to 1 per phase, such as DRIVE.

heuristics: Persistent pressure tool; avoid duplicate casts in same phase.
Most useful when you expect multiple board contests.
